generated_pair.question: What is Amazon Relational Database Service (Amazon RDS)?
generated_pair.answer: Amazon Relational Database Service (Amazon RDS) is a web service that makes it easier to set up, operate, and scale a relational database in the AWS Cloud. It provides cost-efficient, resizable capacity for an industry-standard relational database and manages common database administration tasks.
supporting_sentences: Amazon Relational Database Service (Amazon RDS) is a web service that makes it easier to set up, operate, and scale a relational database in the AWS Cloud. It provides cost-efficient, resizable capacity for an industry-standard relational database and manages common database administration tasks.

generated_pair.question: Which database engines are supported by Amazon RDS?
generated_pair.answer: Amazon RDS supports the following database engines: IBM Db2, MariaDB, Microsoft SQL Server, MySQL, Oracle Database, and PostgreSQL.
supporting_sentences: You can use database engines that you are already familiar with: IBM Db2, MariaDB, Microsoft SQL Server, MySQL, Oracle Database, and PostgreSQL.

generated_pair.question: What are the three types of DB instance storage provided by Amazon RDS?
generated_pair.answer: The three types of DB instance storage provided by Amazon RDS are General Purpose (SSD), Provisioned IOPS (PIOPS), and Magnetic.

generated_pair.question: What is a Multi-AZ deployment in Amazon RDS?
generated_pair.answer: A Multi-AZ deployment is an option where you run your DB instance in several Availability Zones, and Amazon automatically provisions and maintains one or more secondary standby DB instances in a different AZ.
supporting_sentences: You can run your DB instance in several Availability Zones, an option called a Multi-AZ deployment. When you choose this option, Amazon automatically provisions and maintains one or more secondary standby DB instances in a different AZ.

generated_pair.question: Which DB instance classes are supported by Amazon RDS?
generated_pair.answer: Amazon RDS supports the following DB instance class types: General purpose (db.m*), Memory optimized (db.z*, db.x*, db.r*), Compute optimized (db.c*), and Burstable performance (db.t*).
